question: What is Auto Layout? How is it different from frame-based layout?
answer:
Auto Layout is a constraint-based layout engine. You declare relationships between views (e.g. "top of A is 16pt below bottom of B") and the system solves them every layout pass to compute concrete frames.

Vs frame-based:
• Adapts to size classes, dynamic type, rotation, multitasking, RTL.
• Same code works across devices — no per-screen frame math.
• Slower than raw frames at extreme scale (10k+ views), but plenty fast for normal apps.

Three constraint APIs from Apple:
1) NSLayoutConstraint(item:...) — verbose, original.
2) NSLayoutAnchor — fluent, type-safe (modern recommended).
3) UIStackView — composes flow layouts without explicit constraints.

Third-party DSLs (SnapKit, PinLayout) add more sugar but anchors usually suffice.

question: What is intrinsic content size? How does it relate to compression resistance and hugging?
answer:
A view's intrinsic content size is the size it WANTS to be based on its content (e.g. a label fits its text; a button fits its title + insets). Auto Layout uses it as a default constraint.

When there's competition:
• Content hugging priority — how strongly the view resists growing larger than its intrinsic size. Higher = wants to stay small.
• Compression resistance priority — how strongly the view resists shrinking smaller than its intrinsic size. Higher = wants to stay large.

Useful when two labels share a row: give the title low compression resistance and the badge high — title truncates, badge stays full size.

question: How does UIStackView simplify layout?
answer:
UIStackView arranges its arrangedSubviews along an axis (horizontal/vertical) using configurable distribution and alignment — no explicit constraints between siblings needed.

Key properties:
• axis — .horizontal / .vertical
• distribution — .fill, .fillEqually, .fillProportionally, .equalSpacing, .equalCentering
• alignment — .leading, .center, .trailing, .fill (perpendicular axis)
• spacing — fixed gap; or use customSpacing(after:) per child

Nesting stack views models complex layouts cleanly — like flexbox in CSS or Compose's Column/Row. Stack views also handle hidden children automatically (they collapse the slot).

question: What is the safe area? How do you handle it in UIKit?
answer:
Safe area is the region of the screen NOT covered by system UI: status bar, navigation bar, tab bar, home indicator, and notches/cutouts on modern iPhones. Anchoring content to the safe area keeps it visible regardless of device.

• `view.safeAreaLayoutGuide` — UILayoutGuide whose anchors describe the safe area for that view.
• `view.safeAreaInsets` — the inset values (top/leading/bottom/trailing).
• Override `safeAreaInsetsDidChange()` to react.
• Use `additionalSafeAreaInsets` on a UIViewController to push your content past a custom overlay (e.g. a floating banner).

question: How do you debug Auto Layout issues like 'unable to satisfy constraints'?
answer:
When Auto Layout can't find a valid solution it prints a long diagnostic and breaks one constraint. To investigate:

• Read the diagnostic — it lists every involved constraint, with addresses; pause the running app and `po` each constraint to find the offender.
• Identifier — set `constraint.identifier = "contentBottom"` so the log names them.
• Symbolic breakpoint on `UIViewAlertForUnsatisfiableConstraints` — the debugger pauses at the very moment the conflict is detected.
• View Debugger (Xcode → Debug → View Debugging → Capture View Hierarchy) — visualize the tree, inspect constraints, see ambiguous frames in red.
• `view.hasAmbiguousLayout` and `view.exerciseAmbiguityInLayout()` — toggle ambiguous views to spot which one floats.
• Set lower priority (e.g. 999) on optional constraints to mark which one is allowed to break.

question: What is the difference between layoutIfNeeded, setNeedsLayout, and layoutSubviews?
answer:
These three methods coordinate the layout pass:

• `setNeedsLayout()` — marks the view as dirty; the layout engine will lay it out on the next layout cycle (typically before next draw).
• `layoutIfNeeded()` — force an immediate layout pass right now if dirty. Blocking.
• `layoutSubviews()` — the method UIKit calls during the layout pass to position subviews. Override to do custom layout (rare with Auto Layout — usually used for rounded corners, mask paths that depend on size).

A common animation pattern: change constraint constants, then call layoutIfNeeded inside UIView.animate so UIKit interpolates between old and new layouts.
